checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: The dataset contains no AccountsReceivable/ReceivablesNetCurrent concept; only AssetsCurrent is provided (e.g., AssetsCurrent=205109000 (2025-07-26)), which for a supermarket is dominated by cash and inventory, not trade receivables.

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill is flat since the FY2020 acquisition step-up: Goodwill=24190000 (2020-07-25) and Goodwill=24190000 (2025-10-25), i.e., no drift/inflation over five years.

Asset growth is concentrated in tangible PP&E: PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=224890000 (2019-07-27) rising to PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=320549000 (2025-10-25), consistent with capex PaymentsToAcquirePropertyPlantAndEquipment=58765000 (FY2025), not capitalized soft assets.

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: Operating cash flow persistently meets or exceeds net income: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=93222000 vs NetIncomeLoss=56380000 (FY2025); OCF=80849000 vs NI=50462000 (FY2024); OCF=104513000 vs NI=49716000 (FY2023).
The gap is explained by non-cash depreciation, e.g., DepreciationAndAmortization=34398000 (FY2025); this is the healthy direction (cash > earnings), not the earnings-without-cash pattern of concern.

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin is stable: GrossProfit=662966000 on RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=2320690000 (FY2025) = 28.6%, versus GrossProfit=641975000 on Revenue=2236566000 (FY2024) = 28.7%.
Inventory tracks the business: InventoryNet=51424000 (2025-07-26) vs InventoryNet=46739000 (2024-07-27), broadly in line with sales; no anomalous build-up relative to COGS=1657724000 (FY2025).

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Annual net income is materially volatile, not smoothed: NetIncomeLoss=25784000 (FY2013), 5045000 (FY2014), 30620000 (FY2015), 19994000 (FY2021), 49716000 (FY2023) - including a quarterly loss NetIncomeLoss=-3231000 (2022-01-30 to 2022-04-30).

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Current liabilities grow with activity: LiabilitiesCurrent=181389000 (2025-10-25) vs 112474000 (2019-07-27); the decline in LongTermDebtNoncurrent=46290000 (2025-10-25) from 74194000 (2020-07-25) reflects scheduled debt paydown, not reserve release.
No allowance-for-doubtful-accounts or specific reserve concept is provided in the dataset, so a decline-despite-growth pattern in reserves cannot be affirmatively tested.

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Ks are filed on a consistent ~2.5 month cadence after the late-July fiscal year end (e.g., 10-K filing_date 2025-10-09, 2024-10-10, 2023-10-11); no NT 10-K/NT 10-Q late-filing notices appear in the chronology.

Amendments are limited and routine: 10-K/A on 2012-11-05, 2019-10-17 and 2025-11-07, plus an SEC comment-letter exchange (UPLOAD 2022-03-28 / CORRESP 2022-04-08 / UPLOAD 2022-04-27) that resolved without restatement.

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Coverage spans ~15 years of quarterly and annual data (Assets end 2011-07-30 through 2025-10-25), sufficient for trend analysis of revenue, margin, cash flow, inventory and leverage.
Gaps exist for trade receivables and allowance/reserve balances, limiting CL1 and part of CL6, but core income-statement, balance-sheet and cash-flow items are complete.

top_signals: Operating cash flow consistently meets or exceeds net income (OCF=93222000 vs NI=56380000 FY2025), indicating earnings backed by cash
Stable ~28-29% gross margin and inventory tracking sales, consistent with a mature supermarket model
Goodwill flat at 24190000 since FY2020 and asset growth driven by tangible PP&E and lease/acquisition accounting, not soft-asset capitalization
Volatile (non-smoothed) annual earnings and routine, timely filing cadence with only minor, resolved amendments
